metadata.title: Jannis Milz · Software developer, curious by nature
metadata.description: Jannis Milz is a software developer from Switzerland: building eintrittli, coaching gymnastics, and curious by nature.

footer.joke: Printed in Zürich, read wherever you happen to be.

home.headline:
Either I find a way,

or I create one.

letter.p1: Hallo, I’m Jannis, a tinkerer and gymnast from Switzerland. Just like my life motto: I only believe something can’t work once I’ve seriously searched for a way.
letter.p1Note: Also known as stubbornness, but “motto” reads better.
letter.p2: I started programming at an early age; today I do it professionally with passion. Away from the keyboard I’ve been doing gymnastics at my club for more than ten years, and these days I coach the younger kids.
letter.pullQuote: “Experiment until it breaks. Then figure out why.”
letter.figureACaption: Regularly upside down for ten-plus years.

now.title: Now
now.item1: Building <link>eintrittli.ch</link>, an easy to use door check-in system.
now.item3: Volunteer accountant at my gymnastics club.
now.item4: Competed at SwissSkills 2025 in Web Technologies.

projects.title: Projects
projects.eintrittli: A simple check-in system for events and clubs.

closing.p1: Coaching gives me back as much as it demands, by the way: that same thrill of tinkering with a new move until it finally clicks.
closing.p2: The rest of my head belongs to whatever last caught my curiosity: biographies, design, psychology, and the occasional unsolvable math problem.
closing.p2Note: Haven’t solved one yet. The name checks out.
closing.p3: This site is the paper edition of all that. Have a look around, and if something sparks a thought, write me: <mail>hi@jannismilz.com</mail>.
closing.figureBPlaceholder: Photo from coaching the kids, eventually
closing.figureBCaption: Passing on what someone once taught me.

meta.title: Projects
meta.description: Things Jannis Milz has shipped, broken, and shipped again: Eintrittli and other projects.

projects.headline: Shipped, broken, and shipped again.
projects.intro: The projects I’m most proud of: from a live product to school projects that taught me why the live product works.

product.title: Products
product.tagline: Things you can already use today.

eintrittli.description: A simple entry check-in system for events and clubs: configurable forms, data export, and deliberately nothing else. No payments, no login, no fuss.

typst-payqr-swiss.description: A Swiss QR bill generator for Typst.

bbw-heroes.description: A platform where BBW students share their work with others and grow together.

linux-cookbook.description: A guide to the basics of Linux and how to actually use it, written while learning them myself.

swissskills.title: SwissSkills 2025 · Web Technologies
swissskills.body: In 2025, I competed against Switzerland's best young web developers at SwissSkills. I was even interviewed by a newspaper. The short version of my answer made the headlines.
swissskills.figurePlaceholder: Photo from the competition floor, eventually
